display._comment: Stock SV06 Ace (480x272 Goodix) is mounted so the stock firmware drives the panel rotated 180 degrees (ROTATION_180 in the stock mksclient/KlipperScreen config, #986); HelixScreen needs the same to render upright. Seeded into settings.json at install time (top-level display block) so the very first boot frame is oriented correctly, before the wizard runs. Pairs with input.calibration below; if touch ends up inverted on a real unit, verify rotation + matrix together.

calibration._comment: Known-good touch matrix from issue #123 (user R8CEH). Stopgap seed because the first-run touch-calibration math produces a bad matrix on this panel. Read at runtime from /input/calibration/*. This preset is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH (#986): the installer pre-seeds the device-level input + display blocks from here into settings.json before first launch (so the touch-calibration wizard's should_skip() sees a valid matrix and the first frame is correctly rotated); the rest of this preset (printer.*) is applied by the app's runtime preset loader.

leds._selected_strips_comment: Pre-select the camera light so it is controllable as soon as Klipper exposes 'output_pin cameralight'.

printer._hardware_comment: Confirmed stock Klipper object names from published Sovol/R8CEH SV06 Ace configs (#986). The stock printer.cfg uses STANDARD object names ([extruder], [heater_bed], [fan], [heater_fan hotend_fan], [filament_switch_sensor filament_sensor]), which match HelixScreen's built-in defaults -- so preset-mode skipping the heater/fan auto-mapping wizard is safe on this printer.

filament_sensors._comment: Stock filament sensor is a switch-type [filament_switch_sensor filament_sensor] (confirmed #986).

default_macros._comment: Stock SV06 Ace exposes only underscore-internal _LOAD_FILAMENT / _UNLOAD_FILAMENT (no public LOAD_FILAMENT). Confirmed (#986, live Macro.cfg) these are self-contained, parameterless, temp-guarded extrude routines, safe to call directly. Merged over HelixScreen's default_macros so the Load/Unload buttons hit the real macros (HelixScreen heats the hotend before invoking; the macros no-op with a warning below min_extrude_temp).
